ucs.title: [LLM] RedCurl curl.exe upload to legitimate WebDAV/cloud-storage providers (signature exfiltration)
ucs.description: Hunts the tradecraft that gave RedCurl its name: data exfiltration performed by curl.exe (often dropped/renamed and chained behind pcalua.exe or a side-loaded loader) issuing PUT/upload requests against legitimate consumer cloud-storage WebDAV endpoints (cloudme.com, pcloud.com, koofr, 4shared, webdav.yandex.ru). Group-IB consistently observed RedCurl using these providers as low-cost C2/exfil channels because they blend with corporate web traffic.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=false count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name=curl.exe AND Processes.user!="*$" AND (Processes.process="*--upload-file*" OR Processes.process="*-T *" OR Processes.process="*-X PUT*" OR Processes.process="*--data-binary*" OR Processes.process="*webdav*" OR Processes.process="*cloudme.com*" OR Processes.process="*pcloud.com*" OR Processes.process="*koofr*" OR Processes.process="*4shared*" OR Processes.process="*opendrive*" OR Processes.process="*webdav.yandex*" OR Processes.process="*dav.box.com*") by host Processes.user Processes.process Processes.parent_process_name Processes.parent_process Processes.process_id Processes.process_hash | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | eval is_lolbin_proxy=if(match(parent_process_name,"(?i)^(pcalua|cmd|powershell|wscript|cscript|rundll32|mshta)\.exe$"),1,0) | eval is_renamed_curl=if(match(process,"(?i)\\curl\.exe\b")=0 AND match(process,"(?i)curl")=1,1,0) | eval risk=2 + is_lolbin_proxy + is_renamed_curl | where risk>=3 | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | table firstTime lastTime host user process parent_process_name parent_process process_hash risk
ucs.kql:
let _redcurl_cloud_indicators = dynamic(["webdav.yandex","cloudme.com","pcloud.com","my.pcloud.com","koofr.eu","app.koofr.net","4shared.com","webdav.4shared","opendrive.com","webdav.opendrive","dav.box.com","webdav.mail.ru"]);
let _curl_upload_flags = dynamic(["--upload-file","-T ","-X PUT","--data-binary","-F file=@","-u "]);
let _proc = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | where FileName =~ "curl.exe"
        or (InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "curl.exe")
        or (ProcessCommandLine has "curl" and ProcessCommandLine has_any (_redcurl_cloud_indicators))
    | where ProcessCommandLine has_any (_curl_upload_flags)
        or ProcessCommandLine has_any (_redcurl_cloud_indicators)
    | extend LolbinProxy = iff(InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("pcalua.exe","cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","rundll32.exe","mshta.exe","explorer.exe"), 1, 0)
    | extend RenamedCurl = iff(FileName !~ "curl.exe" and (ProcessVersionInfoOriginalFileName =~ "curl.exe" or ProcessVersionInfoProductName has "curl"), 1, 0)
    | project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, FolderPath, SHA256,
              ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
              InitiatingProcessFolderPath, LolbinProxy, RenamedCurl;
_proc
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "curl.exe" or InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "pcalua.exe"
    | where RemoteIPType == "Public"
    | where RemoteUrl has_any (dynamic(["webdav","cloudme","pcloud","koofr","4shared","opendrive","box.com","yandex"]))
    | project DeviceId, NetTime = Timestamp, RemoteUrl, RemoteIP, RemotePort
  ) on $left.DeviceName == $right.DeviceId
| extend Score = 2 + LolbinProxy + RenamedCurl + iff(isnotempty(RemoteUrl), 1, 0)
| where Score >= 3
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, SHA256, ProcessCommandLine,
          InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, RemoteUrl, RemoteIP, Score
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let _redcurl_cloud_indicators = dynamic(["webdav.yandex","cloudme.com","pcloud.com","koofr.eu","app.koofr.net","4shared.com","opendrive.com","dav.box.com","webdav.mail.ru"]);
let _curl_upload_flags = dynamic(["--upload-file","-T ","-X PUT","--data-binary","-F file=@"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| where NewProcessName endswith "\\curl.exe"
    or (CommandLine has "curl" and CommandLine has_any (_redcurl_cloud_indicators))
| where CommandLine has_any (_curl_upload_flags) or CommandLine has_any (_redcurl_cloud_indicators)
| extend LolbinProxy = iff(ParentProcessName has_any ("pcalua.exe","cmd.exe","powershell.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","rundll32.exe","mshta.exe","explorer.exe"), 1, 0)
| extend Score = 2 + LolbinProxy
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, NewProcessName, CommandLine, ParentProcessName, Score
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: RedCurl curl.exe upload to consumer cloud-storage WebDAV provider
id: 9b1c1c3e-redcurl-curl-webdav-exfil
status: experimental
description: Detects RedCurl-style data exfiltration where curl.exe (or curl-renamed binary launched via pcalua/lolbin) issues PUT/upload requests to legitimate consumer cloud-storage WebDAV endpoints (CloudMe, pCloud, Koofr, 4shared, OpenDrive, Box WebDAV, Yandex WebDAV).
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G1039/
  - https://www.group-ib.com/blog/red-curl-2/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.exfiltration
  - attack.t1537
  - attack.t1567.002
  - attack.t1071.001
  - attack.g1039
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  curl_image:
    Image|endswith: '\curl.exe'
  curl_orig:
    OriginalFileName: 'curl.exe'
  upload_flags:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - '--upload-file'
      - ' -T '
      - '-X PUT'
      - '--data-binary'
      - '-F file=@'
  cloud_targets:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'webdav.yandex'
      - 'cloudme.com'
      - 'pcloud.com'
      - 'koofr.eu'
      - 'app.koofr.net'
      - '4shared.com'
      - 'opendrive.com'
      - 'dav.box.com'
      - 'webdav.mail.ru'
  condition: (curl_image or curl_orig) and (upload_flags or cloud_targets)
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate users syncing to personal cloud storage from corporate hosts (rare in regulated SOCs).
  - DevOps tooling that pushes artefacts via WebDAV.
level: high
ucs.rationale: RedCurl's signature is the abuse of consumer cloud-storage providers via curl.exe — Group-IB's two RedCurl reports both anchor on this exact behaviour. Generic 'curl.exe + public IP' detections are too noisy; binding curl-execution to (a) WebDAV PUT verbs AND (b) the specific consumer-cloud hostnames RedCurl repeatedly chooses lifts FP rate to near zero while still catching renamed curl binaries (
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID

ucs.title: [LLM] RedCurl ISO/IMG spearphish container → LNK → cmd/PowerShell stager
ucs.description: Catches RedCurl's distinctive initial-access chain: HR-themed spearphish (CV, bonus letter, contract amendment) delivers a mountable .iso/.img/.vhd container which the user double-clicks; explorer mounts it, the embedded LNK fires cmd.exe → PowerShell which invokes RedCurl's PowerShell dropper (the 'RedCurl.dropper'). Time-correlates the container drop with the script child within 1 hour on the same host.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=false count min(_time) as drop_time from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where (Filesystem.file_name="*.iso" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.img" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.vhd" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.vhdx") AND Filesystem.process_name IN (outlook.exe,olk.exe,chrome.exe,msedge.exe,firefox.exe,brave.exe,winrar.exe,7zg.exe,7zfm.exe) by host Filesystem.user Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.file_path Filesystem.process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | rename file_name as container_name file_path as container_path process_name as delivery_app | join type=inner host [ | tstats summariesonly=false count min(_time) as exec_time from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name=explorer.exe AND Processes.process_name IN (cmd.exe,powershell.exe,pwsh.exe,wscript.exe,cscript.exe,rundll32.exe,mshta.exe,pcalua.exe) AND (Processes.process="*.lnk*" OR match(Processes.parent_process,"(?i)[D-Z]:\\\\")) by host Processes.user Processes.process Processes.process_name Processes.parent_process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename process as child_cmdline process_name as child_proc parent_process as parent_cmd ] | where exec_time>=drop_time AND (exec_time-drop_time)<=3600 | eval delay_sec=exec_time-drop_time | table drop_time exec_time delay_sec host user delivery_app container_name container_path child_proc child_cmdline parent_cmd
ucs.kql:
let _container_ext = dynamic([".iso",".img",".vhd",".vhdx"]);
let _delivery_apps = dynamic(["outlook.exe","olk.exe","chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","brave.exe","winrar.exe","7zfm.exe","7zg.exe"]);
let _stager_children = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","rundll32.exe","mshta.exe","pcalua.exe"]);
let ContainerDrops = DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where ActionType == "FileCreated"
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (_delivery_apps)
    | where FileName has_any (_container_ext)
    | project DeviceId, DeviceName, ContainerTime = Timestamp,
              ContainerName = FileName, ContainerPath = FolderPath,
              DeliveryApp = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              RecipientUser = InitiatingProcessAccountName;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "explorer.exe"
| where FileName in~ (_stager_children)
| where ProcessCommandLine has ".lnk"
    or InitiatingProcessFolderPath matches regex @"(?i)^[D-Z]:\\"
    or ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\b[D-Z]:\\.*\.(lnk|cmd|bat|ps1|vbs|js)\b"
| join kind=inner ContainerDrops on DeviceId
| where Timestamp between (ContainerTime .. ContainerTime + 1h)
| extend DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', Timestamp, ContainerTime)
| extend SuspiciousCmd = iff(
    ProcessCommandLine has_any ("-enc","-EncodedCommand","-w hidden","-windowstyle hidden",
                                 "DownloadString","IEX","Invoke-Expression",
                                 "Invoke-WebRequest","Net.WebClient","FromBase64String",
                                 "Start-BitsTransfer","curl ","certutil"), 1, 0)
| project Timestamp, ContainerTime, DelaySec, DeviceName, AccountName,
          DeliveryApp, ContainerName, ContainerPath,
          ChildProcess = FileName, ChildCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
          Parent = InitiatingProcessFileName, ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          MountedFolder = InitiatingProcessFolderPath, SuspiciousCmd
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let _container_ext = dynamic([".iso",".img",".vhd",".vhdx"]);
let _delivery_apps = dynamic(["outlook.exe","chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","brave.exe","winrar.exe","7zfm.exe","7zg.exe"]);
let _stager_children = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","rundll32.exe","mshta.exe","pcalua.exe"]);
let Containers = ImFileEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
    | where EventType == "FileCreated"
    | where ActingProcessName has_any (_delivery_apps)
    | where TargetFileName has_any (_container_ext)
    | project DvcHostname, ContainerTime = TimeGenerated,
              ContainerName = TargetFileName, ContainerPath = TargetFilePath,
              DeliveryApp = ActingProcessName;
ImProcessCreate
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where ActorUsername !endswith "$"
| where ParentProcessName =~ "explorer.exe"
| where TargetProcessName has_any (_stager_children)
| where TargetProcessCommandLine has ".lnk"
    or TargetProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\b[D-Z]:\\.*\.(lnk|cmd|bat|ps1|vbs|js)\b"
| join kind=inner Containers on DvcHostname
| where TimeGenerated between (ContainerTime .. ContainerTime + 1h)
| extend DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', TimeGenerated, ContainerTime)
| extend SuspiciousCmd = iff(TargetProcessCommandLine has_any ("-enc","-EncodedCommand","-w hidden","DownloadString","IEX","Invoke-WebRequest","Net.WebClient","FromBase64String","curl ","certutil"), 1, 0)
| project TimeGenerated, ContainerTime, DelaySec, DvcHostname, ActorUsername,
          DeliveryApp, ContainerName, TargetProcessName, TargetProcessCommandLine, SuspiciousCmd
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.rationale: RedCurl's reported delivery method (Group-IB 2020 + 2023) is overwhelmingly an HR/legal-themed spearphish carrying a mountable disk image. The chain — browser/Outlook drops .iso → user mounts → explorer launches LNK → cmd/PowerShell on a non-C: drive letter — is highly atypical for legitimate corporate workflows, especially when a mailclient or browser was the dropper. Most generic 'ISO mount' det
